message:
feat(web): implement WebSocket upgrade for /debug/ws endpoint

The debug server's /debug/ws endpoint previously returned a 501 stub
with a "not implemented (Phase 0)" JSON error. This commit implements
a minimal RFC 6455-compliant WebSocket upgrade that pushes a single
runtime snapshot frame and closes cleanly:

- Add read_headers() to parse HTTP headers from the request after the
  request line, needed for WebSocket handshake validation
- Add header_value() helper for case-insensitive header lookup
- Add websocket_accept_key() implementing the SHA-1 + base64 accept
  key derivation per RFC 6455 Section 4.2.2
- Add write_ws_text_frame() supporting payloads up to 2^63 bytes via
  the 7-bit / 16-bit / 64-bit length encoding
- Add handle_websocket() that validates Upgrade/Connection/Version
  headers, sends 101 Switching Protocols response, pushes one JSON
  snapshot as a text frame, then sends a close frame (0x88 0x00)
- Update the /debug/ws route to call handle_websocket() and fall back
  to a 400 JSON error on upgrade failure (replacing the old 501)
- Add 400 "Bad Request" to write_response status map
- Update test from asserting 501 to performing a real WebSocket
  handshake and verifying the 101 response + frame payload

The one-shot push design keeps the endpoint lightweight while enabling
programmatic snapshot retrieval via any WebSocket client.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
